Coriflora is a genus of flowering plants, commonly known as the "native violet", that have been used in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of Coriflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Coriflora include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including colds, flu, and digestive issues.
